column1: what is socat
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: Socat is a command-line utility for bidirectional data transfer between two independent data channels.

column1: how to install socat on ubuntu
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: Install socat on Ubuntu with 'sudo apt install socat'.

column1: explain socat options -d -d
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: The -d option increases verbosity, and -d -d prints even more detailed debug information.

column1: what does fork do in socat
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: The fork option makes socat handle each new connection in a separate child process, allowing multiple simultaneous connections.

column1: socat vs netcat comparison
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: Socat is more feature-rich than netcat, supporting SSL, proxies, and various address types.

column1: what is the difference between socat and netcat
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: Netcat is simpler for basic TCP/UDP, while socat offers advanced options like SSL, UNIX sockets, and more.

column1: how to use socat for file transfer
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: Use socat to send a file: on receiver: 'socat TCP-LISTEN:1234 OPEN:file.txt,creat', on sender: 'socat OPEN:file.txt TCP:receiver:1234'.

column1: can socat do SSL encryption
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: Yes, socat supports SSL encryption using OPENSSL addresses.

column1: what are some common socat use cases
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: Common uses include port forwarding, creating tunnels, transferring files, and debugging network services.

column1: how to check if socat is installed
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: Run 'which socat' or 'socat -V' to check if socat is installed.

column1: socat man page summary
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: The socat man page details all address types, options, and examples; use 'man socat' to view it.

column1: what is the syntax of socat
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.

column1: how to create a simple echo server with socat
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: Run 'socat TCP-LISTEN:8080,fork EXEC:cat' to create an echo server.

column1: explain the reuseaddr option in socat
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: reuseaddr allows binding to a port even if it is in TIME_WAIT state, useful for quick restarts.

column1: what is the purpose of the fork option
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: fork makes socat handle each connection in a child process, enabling multiple clients to connect simultaneously.

column1: how to forward multiple ports with socat
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: Run separate socat instances for each port, e.g., in background jobs or a script.

column1: does socat support UDP
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: Yes, socat supports UDP with addresses like UDP-LISTEN and UDP-CONNECT.

column1: how to use socat with UNIX sockets
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: Use UNIX-LISTEN for server and UNIX-CONNECT for client, e.g., 'socat UNIX-LISTEN:/tmp/sock,fork TCP:localhost:80'.

column1: what is the difference between TCP-LISTEN and TCP-CONNECT
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: TCP-LISTEN creates a server waiting for connections, while TCP-CONNECT initiates a connection to a remote host.

column1: how to log traffic with socat
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: Use the -v option to print data to stderr, or combine with tee to save to a file.

column1: socat timeout option explanation
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: The -T option sets a timeout in seconds for inactivity on a connection.

column1: how to use socat as a simple proxy
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: Run 'socat TCP-LISTEN:8080,fork TCP:proxy-server:3128' to forward to a proxy.

column1: what are the address types in socat
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: Address types include TCP, UDP, UNIX, OPENSSL, PTY, FILE, EXEC, and many more.

column1: how to forward traffic between two interfaces with socat
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: Use 'socat INTERFACE:eth0,fork INTERFACE:eth1' to bridge traffic between interfaces.

column1: can socat handle IPv6 addresses
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: Yes, use the -6 option or specify IPv6 addresses in brackets.

column1: what is the -T option in socat
column2: You are the ShellMaster. Category: general.
column6: ShellMaster: The -T option sets a total inactivity timeout for the connection.

column1: how to create a tunnel that survives disconnects

column6: ShellMaster: Use a script that restarts socat on failure, or combine with tools like systemd.

column1: socat examples for network debugging

column6: ShellMaster: Example: 'socat -v TCP-LISTEN:8080,fork TCP:google.com:80' to inspect HTTP traffic.
